checks_reviewed.evidence: Confirmed repo rules for TDD, no secrets/customer identifiers, mobile-mcp policy, runtime verification, contracts SoT, and PR gates.
Confirmed runtime versions, required MCP list, Expo plugin-provided MCP section, Stitch gcloud prerequisites, Railway context, and CI/local harness implications.
Confirmed pinned repo MCP config for mobile-mcp, serena, and stitch.
Confirmed post-clone setup, EAS human-gate ownership, and npx eas-cli init context.
Confirmed EXPO_TOKEN, store credential, and secret handling boundaries.
Confirmed Railway install/login/evidence workflow, secret redaction rules, and railway setup agent boundary.
Confirmed prior reviewer evidence supports the MCP classification and target-session auth caveat.
Confirmed prior corrected prompt identifies optional MCPs, auxiliary CLIs, and secret-like Tavily URL handling without reproducing the value.
Confirmed plan scope, guide structure, implementation steps, human gates, and acceptance criteria are aligned with local SoT.
Confirmed dirty worktree; plan accounts for unrelated changes and does not require clean baseline.

Confirmed required MCPs are enabled in this reviewer session. Expo auth appeared session-dependent here, matching the plan's target-session verification caveat. Secret-like Tavily URL was not reproduced.
Confirmed Railway CLI version 5.6.2 availability; login was not exercised in this read-only review.
Confirmed Google Cloud SDK 554.0.0 availability; ADC/account commands were not exercised in this read-only review.
Confirmed the plan's expected local state: EAS CLI is not available in the mobile workspace.
This review inspected a plan only and made no runtime artifact edits. The future implementation plan requires this when PROJECT_ENVIRONMENT.md or applicable runtime SoT changes.
This review made no Codex runtime or local-harness-covered implementation changes. The future implementation plan includes conditional harness verification.
No app, backend, package, or runtime implementation changed in this plan review.

residual_risks: Expo MCP auth state is session-specific; the guide must require codex mcp list verification in the actual target Codex environment.
Railway whoami and gcloud ADC/project/service enablement were intentionally not exercised here; the future guide must keep them as target-machine verification steps.
External official docs for Expo/EAS, Railway, Google Cloud, and Codex MCP help were not revalidated in this plan review; the future guide implementation step explicitly requires that check.
The worktree is dirty with unrelated changes, so future implementation must avoid implying a clean baseline or reverting unrelated files.
